message:
fix(ft-nn): MultiMarginLoss + SoftMarginLoss compose through autograd primitives [bd-1s4q, bd-fjao]

Two more children of frankentorch-cq0b (ft-nn loss-severance
umbrella) — both used to extract values, compute the per-element
loss in plain f64, and rebuild a non-grad leaf.

MultiMarginLoss [bd-1s4q]:
  Compose through one_hot(target) + mul + sub + relu + pow +
  sum_dim + div — same recipe as the session multi_margin_loss
  fix (frankentorch-x9cr) but with the Reduction enum's None /
  Mean / Sum dispatch at the end.

SoftMarginLoss [bd-fjao]:
  Compose through neg + mul + relu + abs + log1p + exp + add —
  same recipe as the session soft_margin_loss fix (frankentorch-
  tr2a). With z = -t * x, loss_per_elem = relu(z) + log1p(exp(-|z|)),
  numerically stable for both signs of z.

Closes frankentorch-1s4q + frankentorch-fjao. ft-nn lib tests:
517 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-1le","title":"Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild","description":"## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:56:40.262095265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096361407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096186135Z","close_reason":"Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1nn","title":"Enforce index_add/index_copy shape + rank rules","description":"Audit: tensor_index_add/index_copy accept mismatched src/index shapes and non-1D indices; they silently ignore extra indices. Fix by enforcing index rank=1 and src shape matches input except dim, with src_dim == index length. Add tests for mismatch errors and f32 parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:57:29.258566505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230978644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230629796Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1s2","title":"Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes","description":"Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:49:10.441649598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804652781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804379905Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-1s4q","title":"Fix ft-nn MultiMarginLoss autograd via one_hot + relu + pow [child of cq0b]","description":"ft-nn MultiMarginLoss::forward at lib.rs:11852 has the same per-sample severance pattern as the session multi_margin_loss method (already fixed under frankentorch-x9cr).

Compose through session.one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div for per-sample loss, then apply the Reduction enum's mean/sum/none.","notes":"Fixed via composition through one_hot + tensor_mul + tensor_sub + tensor_relu + tensor_pow + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_div primitives — same recipe as session multi_margin_loss (frankentorch-x9cr) but with the Reduction enum dispatch at the end. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:19:38.039423136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:21:57.018143509Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:21:57.015734527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1tax","title":"Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp","description":"These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.","notes":"All children resolved across multiple commits: cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile/logit/xlogy/xlog1py/entr composed through autograd primitives in earlier rounds; gammaln (7cc0b45), digamma (86fb29d), polygamma + multigammaln (bd2817b), erfinv (ac049c6), logcumsumexp (ec75d35) wrapped in tensor_apply_function with analytical backwards. ft-api lib tests now at 1135 passing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:24:23.438424203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.517650830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T21:29:34.515232671Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2med","title":"Severed-autograd loss umbrella: focal, poisson_nll, gaussian_nll","description":"Three more loss functions in ft-api that extract values, compute the per-element loss in plain f64, build a requires_grad=false leaf, then take tensor_mean — same severed-autograd pattern recently fixed for cross_entropy_with_smoothing / multilabel_soft_margin / soft_margin / multi_margin. The tensor_mean step preserves autograd from that point but the prior leaf has already severed the tape — so input never receives gradient.

Affected:
  - focal_loss (lib.rs:10324) — used in dense object detection (RetinaNet etc.)
  - poisson_nll_loss (lib.rs:10357) — count regression
  - gaussian_nll_loss (lib.rs:10398) — variational / probabilistic regression

Each has a closed-form composition through autograd-aware primitives; Stirling / 2pi correction terms are constants and can be added as scalars without affecting the tape.

Children: each loss gets its own fix bead. Surfaced during continuing /reality-check pass.","notes":"All children closed: poisson_nll (zl3n, 8d33f51), gaussian_nll (vjow, this commit), focal_loss (m6uk, this commit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:39:48.922194244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:54.140379643Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:45:54.137798514Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-etu","title":"Investigate and close next verified parity gap from FEATURE_PARITY","description":"No open beads remain. Use current source + FEATURE_PARITY + remote quality gates to identify one still-missing or broken FrankenTorch parity surface, implement it, verify it, and sync beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:33:53.342830285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194397110Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:25.194152728Z","close_reason":"Added conformance coverage for cov/corrcoef/mode/quantile in tensor_advanced fixtures and runner; verified targeted ft-conformance test passes remotely; workspace check passes remotely; remaining fmt/clippy failures tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-f6nn","title":"ft-nn PReLU and shrink activations sever autograd","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs after the recent activation-gradient repair wave. Several activation modules still read tensor values, compute in plain f64, and return session.tensor_variable(..., false), so a requires_grad=true input produces a fresh untracked output and tensor_backward cannot propagate gradients. Concrete sites: PReLU::forward lines 3576-3642 reads both input and its learnable weight via tensor_values and returns an untracked leaf, so both input gradients and the PReLU weight gradient are dropped; Softshrink lines 3918-3938, Hardshrink lines 3966-3978, Tanhshrink lines 3991-4000, Softsign lines 4014-4023, Softmin lines 4046-4057, and RReLU lines 4154-4168 have the same severed-input pattern. Existing tests for these modules are forward-only (for example prelu_default_forward/prelu_has_parameters/prelu_multichannel, softshrink_shrinks, tanhshrink_values, softsign_bounded, softmin_is_softmax_of_negated, rrelu_module_*), and the full ft-nn test suite still passes: rch exec -- cargo test -p ft-nn reported 508 passed on 2026-04-29. Repro by code inspection: create a requires_grad=true input, run PReLU::forward (or any listed activation), reduce with tensor_sum, then tensor_backward; tensor_gradient for input is absent because the forward output is a requires_grad=false leaf. For PReLU, tensor_gradient for self.weight is also absent even though parameters() exposes that learnable slope. Fix by composing each activation through existing autograd-aware primitives (e.g. PReLU via tensor_where/tensor_mul with broadcasted weight; shrink ops via tensor_where; Softmin via tensor_neg or 0-input subtraction then tensor_softmax; RReLU via leaky_relu-style composition) and add regression tests asserting input gradients, plus weight gradients for PReLU.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:09:37.892015821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.745564147Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:53:11.743783997Z","close_reason":"Composed all 7 listed activation modules through autograd-aware primitives: PReLU via tensor_reshape+tensor_expand+tensor_mul+tensor_where (now propagates to both input and learnable weight); Softshrink/Hardshrink via nested tensor_where + tensor_abs/tensor_sub/tensor_add + tensor_gt/tensor_lt; Tanhshrink via tensor_sub(input, tanh(input)); Softsign via tensor_div(input, 1+abs); Softmin via tensor_neg + tensor_softmax; RReLU via where(x>=0, x, slope*x). Added 8 gradient regression tests asserting analytical d/dx for each module (plus PReLU weight grads, both single-slope and multichannel). All 11 existing forward tests continue to pass — forward parity preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-fjao","title":"Fix ft-nn SoftMarginLoss autograd via composition [child of cq0b]","description":"ft-nn SoftMarginLoss uses the same numerically-stable log1p(exp(-t*x)) formula as the session soft_margin_loss method (already fixed under tr2a). Compose through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp + Reduction enum dispatch.","notes":"Fixed via composition through neg/mul/relu/abs/log1p/exp + Reduction dispatch — mirrors the session soft_margin_loss fix (tr2a). ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:20:49.719321611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:21:57.371278073Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:21:57.368438176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fntr","title":"softmax / log_softmax lack subprocess conformance against torch/numpy","description":"tensor_softmax and tensor_log_softmax are the canonical activation/output functions for classification heads, attention layers, and most loss formulations. They have unit tests in ft-api but no subprocess conformance against an authoritative reference (torch.nn.functional.softmax / scipy.special.softmax / numpy). Bit-exact agreement matters because:

- Numerical stability: log_softmax(x) = log(softmax(x)) is computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid exp overflow; the equivalence with the naive form must hold within libm precision across all inputs.
- Gradient stability: softmax/log_softmax forward/backward are coupled (dL/dx = softmax(x) - target for cross-entropy). Drift between FrankenTorch and PyTorch here corrupts every classification training run.
- Edge cases: large positive inputs (exp overflow risk), large negative (underflow), uniform input (all equal probabilities), single-element softmax (degenerate).

Add torch_softmax_torch_subprocess_conformance covering interior, large-magnitude, near-uniform, and edge-case inputs along multiple dims; assert ULP-tolerant equality against scipy.special.softmax (or numpy formulation if scipy unavailable). Same for log_softmax.

Bias toward filing-from-/reality-check: this is a pre-existing instance test gap, not a new bug.","notes":"Closed by adding torch_softmax_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering interior, uniform, scalar, large-positive / large-negative (with max-subtract numerical stability), mixed-sign, and 1-D / 2-D / 3-D shapes across multiple dims. 8-ULP tolerance with 1e-15 absolute floor. Also surfaced the log_softmax precision bug tracked under frankentorch-ebrb (now fixed in the same commit).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:22:44.964172677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.625884901Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T22:26:01.622244776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-fq9","title":"Add trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner to ft-api","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:31:01.289432681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.881175699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:53.880929192Z","close_reason":"Implemented trapezoid, cumulative_trapezoid, addr, inner with 9 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-frq7","title":"Implement tensor_linalg_inv autograd: grad_A = -A_inv^T grad_out A_inv^T [child of tw0y]","description":"Wrap tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the standard matrix-calculus backward:
    grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T   where Y = A^{-1}
Save Y (the forward output) for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse. Output shape is [n, n] for n×n input. F64-only autograd path; F32 input with requires_grad continues to fail-loud (consistent with the affine_grid pattern).

Add tensor_linalg_inv_propagates_gradient regression test in ft-api: for diagonal A, the inverse is reciprocal-diag and the gradient under sum loss simplifies to a clean closed-form for verification.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_linalg_inv in tensor_apply_function with the analytical backward grad_A = -Y^T @ grad_Y @ Y^T where Y = A^{-1}. Y is saved for backward to avoid recomputing the inverse; the matmul chain is implemented inline in the closure (n×n is small enough). Added linalg_inv_propagates_gradient_via_inv_transpose_outer regression test (diagonal A=[[2,0],[0,4]] under sum loss → grad = [-1/4,-1/8,-1/8,-1/16]). ft-api lib tests: 1140.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T18:04:39.351961113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:06:46.241906978Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:06:46.239539378Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
